    1. Michele Boyer

      Hi Ali. Because we’re a household of two, it’s difficult for me to make the recipe using the pan called for. For years now, I make the recipe and divide the batter between two large loaf pans. I bake both. Once they’ve cooled, My husband and I enjoy one and freeze the other for later. It doesn’t have that super crispy crust after it’s defrosted, but it’s still delicious.

      April 20, 2022 at 9:25 pm Reply
      1. Ali

        Thank you! I went through all the comments and saw some people baked it in two 8in pans as well. I can’t do muffins, sounds like too much work lol

        April 21, 2022 at 5:08 am Reply
  393. Lise Corson

    Hi Deb,
    Being a prolific baker, home cook I’ve made my share of coffee cakes and am experienced in the ways of baking/cooking. I’ve never seen whipped egg whites/meringue folded in to a coffee cake batter. I’m intrigued. What is the reason for this? What does it “do” for the cake?
    thanks so much! Love your recipes, books and videos!!!

    April 25, 2022 at 8:18 am Reply
    1. deb

      It gives cakes more a lightness and lift. It’s definitely worth it here.

      April 25, 2022 at 11:50 am Reply
  394. Chris

    This popped up as popular right now. Are we related, Deb? Because this is also my family’s recipe dating back to the early 70’s(?). It is THE cake recipe most requested for every occasion. The go-to recipe for bringing to dinners, church socials, funerals. We also had a version with ground walnuts but not nearly as good. We lived in Queens. I believe my mom got the recipe from her best friend and the story was that it won a big bake-off contest though I’ve never found out if that is true. It’s the best, glad you shared so many more people can now indulge!!

    April 25, 2022 at 5:02 pm Reply
  395. Lucy

    This is hands down my favourite every-day cake recipe! Everyone I make it for raves about it, and I keep extra slices frozen. It freezes / defrosts extremely well, so I love to have some on hand for when I get a craving.

    May 16, 2022 at 2:47 pm Reply
  396. Laura

    I love this cake! I would like to make in an 8 or 9 inch square pan. Any way to scale down easily???

    June 3, 2022 at 12:30 pm Reply
    1. deb

      Yes, you can halve everything!

      June 4, 2022 at 9:46 am Reply
  397. Laura

    I’ve made this quite a few times. This time for some reason my middle layer of cinnamon sugar/chocolate chips kind of disappeared into the rest of the cake. Any idea why? Still super yummy!!!

    June 4, 2022 at 7:09 pm Reply
  398. Laura Clark

    Delicious!! Do you have the nutritional info for this dessert? Thanks😊

    July 29, 2022 at 1:14 pm Reply
  399. Emily

    Would there be any adjustments for using a glass pan?

    August 23, 2022 at 6:56 am Reply
    1. deb

      I’d bake it at 25 degrees less.

      August 23, 2022 at 11:31 am Reply
  400. Marci Lebenbom

    Can you freeze this cake?

    August 24, 2022 at 12:57 pm Reply
    1. Michele

      Yes, you absolutely can freeze this cake. The top isn’t as crispy as it is first from the oven. Since we are a family of two, I make the full recipe and divide it between two smaller aluminum tins. I bake both at the same time. Then we eat one tin over a couple days and freeze the other. It lasts for several months. I currently have three tins in my freezer. I should pull one out for tonight!

      August 24, 2022 at 2:10 pm Reply
  401. Casey

    Made this yesterday (exactly as described) and it is delicious! Will be keeping this on my list of recipes to repeat, I think it is even better on day 2.

    September 5, 2022 at 6:21 pm Reply
  402. Giorgiana

    What are your thoughts on making it into muffins? Freezing them and taking out servings as needed? Iv made it as a cake several times and love it!

    September 10, 2022 at 7:25 pm Reply
  403. Gwen Werman

    Can the sour cream coffee cake be frozen? I know you said you never froze it. It’s been a while and was wondering if anyone had frozen it and how did it turn out?

    October 2, 2022 at 5:04 pm Reply
    1. LUCY

      Yes! So good frozen

      February 10, 2024 at 3:36 pm Reply
      1. Michele Boyer

        I freeze this cake all the time. The recipe is too much for my husband and I, so I divide the recipe between two pans. After they bake and cool down, we enjoy one and freeze the other. It’s delicious once it thaws. I currently have one in the freezer. Enjoy.

        February 10, 2024 at 3:45 pm Reply
  404. Paul

    Why butter the baking pan when you use parchment paper? What am i missing?

    October 6, 2022 at 3:40 pm Reply
    1. deb

      For the sides and corners. The parchment makes it easier to take out.

      October 6, 2022 at 3:45 pm Reply
  405. Sue Sober

    I would like to bake this in a tube or cake pan for a tall cake .Any suggestions what size pan?

    January 2, 2023 at 2:49 pm Reply
  406. Sue Sober

    I would like to make this in a tube or regular cake pan for a taller cake.
    Can you suggest what pan size to use?

    January 2, 2023 at 2:51 pm Reply
  407. Anat

    I was looking for a light cake but with the consistency of crumb cake , this recipe is easy to follow and the cake is delicious.
    Thank you for sharing

    February 10, 2023 at 6:13 am Reply
  408. Sue Sober

    I found my cake a bit dry . Baked it 40 minutes . Is it necessary to whip the egg whites?

    February 23, 2023 at 7:43 am Reply
  409. Aisha

    Made a half batch in an 8″ square pan (used 2 eggs, tossed off a bit) with the addition of lemon zest and maldon sprinkled in with the sugar, and some black pepper on top. It has a lovely texture, the zest and salt balance out the sweetness for my taste, but I would cut back on the chocolate chips next time — you can barely taste the cake!

    July 4, 2023 at 12:38 pm Reply
  410. M

    Delicious! For those wondering about a muffin variation, I made a third of the recipe and divided the batter into 8 muffins. Probably could have gotten away with 6, but this gave a nice batter to cinnamon sugar/ chocolate chip ratio. Didn’t measure the chocolate chips, just sprinkled them on. Used whole milk Greek yogurt instead of sour cream and the texture was lovely.

    August 14, 2023 at 10:58 am Reply
  411. Karina Spillman

    Can you use non fat Greek yogurt as a substitute?

    October 5, 2023 at 12:29 pm Reply
  412. Ilyse Smith

    I am going to half the recipe as we are only 2 in the house. Do you think 8×8 pan is good? Maybe reduce the cooking time.
    Also, always at a dilemma with the half portion of eggs???

    October 6, 2023 at 2:21 am Reply
    1. deb

      You can use an 8×8 or 9-inch round. Normally I’d just whisk an egg up and eyeball pouring in half, but this would require more splitting hairs! There’s probably no harm in using a full extra egg yolk?

      October 6, 2023 at 11:23 am Reply
  413. Jay

    This is the World Famous “Aunt Betty’s” chocolate chip sour cream coffee cake recipe.

    October 6, 2023 at 5:29 am Reply
  414. Jacqueline McAllister

    I am currently living in Cozumel, Mexico. Sour cream just isn’t that easy to find. And once someone does find it, they post the “news” and all of us are racing to the store to get it.

    Is it possible to use Natural Greek Yogurt?

    October 7, 2023 at 1:38 am Reply
  415. Poppy

    Wonderful but too much chocolate. Will be trying 6-8oz.

    October 7, 2023 at 7:55 pm Reply
  416. Rose Karlebach

    Delicious coffee cake. My husband grew up Jewish in NYC and remembers going into Manhattan with his parents to visit us Oma and Omama. When he bit into it, he said it reminded him of his childhood! Thank you!

    December 31, 2023 at 1:59 pm Reply
  417. Janice

    This was a lot of work but worth it. I added chopped pecans to the top cinnamon/sugar layer. It’s especially yummy slightly warm from the oven!

    January 6, 2024 at 10:27 am Reply
  418. Lucy

    This is my favourite cake I have ever made for every day. Everyone I make it for absolutely raves about it. It also freezes really well in slices to save for later

    February 10, 2024 at 3:35 pm Reply
  419. Anna

    Hi! Could I omit the chocolate chips and keep the rest of the recipe as is for similarly great results?

    April 13, 2024 at 3:10 pm Reply
  420. Sicktastic

    I made this paleo and with dairy free yogurt. I used Bob’s Red Mill Paleo Baking Flour & Kite Hill plain almond yogurt. Because I adore cinnamon, I tossed some in the batter. This coffee cake is a dream 😍🥰😍
    Grain free!

    May 4, 2024 at 12:13 am Reply
    1. Sicktastic

      PS I made this in a 9×9 metal pan because “I wish I was a little bit taller, I wish I was a baller…”
      Also, if you use the whisk attachment after whipping the egg whites to make everything else, you will win. It adds more air and fun to this coffee cake.

      May 4, 2024 at 12:21 am Reply
  421. Kara Nierenberg

    I just made this cake for the umpteenth time and it is still delicious! But I did think I can handle making this while multi-tasking and I was quickly reminded that this is a vintage SK recipe from back before Deb and I had kids. RIP life where multiple extra bowls didn’t bother me. Anyone make this before WITHOUT whipping the egg whites? Still tasty?

    July 23, 2024 at 1:35 pm Reply
  422. Cathy

    I have made this many times and it is outstanding. has anyone tried to freeze this?

    September 28, 2024 at 4:38 pm Reply
  423. Tricia L

    Do you think this would do OK with gluten-free flour blends?

    October 10, 2024 at 4:35 pm Reply
    1. Juanita

      Yes! I use a paleo flour blend of almond flour, coconut flour and arrow root starch and it works great!

      October 10, 2024 at 7:57 pm Reply
    2. deb

      I haven’t made it gluten-free but here are comments from people who have and report back on what they did:
      https://smittenkitchen.com/2006/11/chocolate-chip-sour-cream-cake/#comment-598456
      https://smittenkitchen.com/2006/11/chocolate-chip-sour-cream-cake/#comment-598504
      https://smittenkitchen.com/2006/11/chocolate-chip-sour-cream-cake/#comment-868281
      https://smittenkitchen.com/2006/11/chocolate-chip-sour-cream-cake/#comment-1554854
      https://smittenkitchen.com/2006/11/chocolate-chip-sour-cream-cake/#comment-1601680
      https://smittenkitchen.com/2006/11/chocolate-chip-sour-cream-cake/#comment-1916090

      October 11, 2024 at 11:19 am Reply
      1. Tricia

        Thank you! I wish I’d thought to do control + f for gluten before I asked, because then I saw other replies like this. Sorry to make more work for you! I’ll be making this soon for a gf friend!

        October 11, 2024 at 2:41 pm Reply
  424. Simone Aridas

    Thanks so much for the coffee cake recipe looking forward to trying it

    October 11, 2024 at 3:45 am Reply
  425. Michelle

    Incredible! I think next time, I may add some walnuts or pecans in the cinamon mixture. What a wonderful recipe.

    October 13, 2024 at 5:45 pm Reply
  426. Laura K.

    This is absolutely delicious. I made it once so far but I’m starting to miss it, so I’ll have to make it again soon. This cake just hits the spot for me with a cup of hot coffee in the morning. Thank you for sharing 😊

    November 25, 2024 at 7:05 pm Reply
  427. Michelle

    So, so very good. I added some chopped pecans to give it a little crunch with the chocolate chips. This is my second time making this – it is an amazing recipe.

    December 31, 2024 at 9:39 pm Reply
  428. Julie

    I’ve been looking at this recipe for years and decided 2025 was the year I’d finally make it. I wish I hadn’t waited so long – it’s absolutely delicious! I followed everything exactly as written and it turned out perfectly ❤️

    January 1, 2025 at 9:09 pm Reply
  429. Deanna Olson

    This cake is crazy delicious! I usually stay away from making cakes- I never get the texture right, as I need to make them gluten free. But this recipe is perfect- I used Bob’s Red Mill gf flour. I also halved it and baked it in an 8×8 cake pan, it baked for 35 minutes. I didn’t bother using parchment paper, as I am serving and storing it in the pan. It came out looking just like Deb’s! 💜

    April 2, 2025 at 1:28 pm Reply
  430. Lauren

    The flavour and texture of this cake are fantastic – but I had a couple of issues with it.
    Firstly, the cake baked up with some holes in it – any idea why?
    Secondly, I seemed to have way more chocolate chips on mine than in the pictures, despite measuring them! Next time I’d reduce the amount.

    April 20, 2025 at 3:02 pm Reply
  431. Nava

    hi! looks so good! you recommend using all purpose flour, is it better then cake flour? thank you 🙏

    May 10, 2025 at 4:30 pm Reply
    1. deb

      I use all-purpose flour for simplicity — I don’t like stocking both. To me, this cake is perfectly tender. However, if you’d like to swap with cake flour, it should work fine (just make sure it’s not the self-rising kind). You can also make your own cake flour substitute.

      May 12, 2025 at 1:58 pm Reply
  432. Holly Ramer

    I didn’t have any sour cream or chocolate chips yet felt I HAD to make this today 😂. So I used Greek yogurt and replaced the chocolate chips with roughly two cups of chopped dark chocolate, white chocolate and dried cherries. Yum!

    May 11, 2025 at 10:13 am Reply
  433. Diana

    The texture of this cake is so good and the flavor is mostly good but I’m getting a somewhat strong baking soda aftertaste. Has anyone else noticed this? I wonder if I could decrease the baking soda but wouldn’t want to try if it’ll mess up the rise.

    May 23, 2025 at 3:39 pm Reply
  434. Rachel Laniado

    Just made this for the second time. It may be my favourite cake of all time!
    It’s moan out loud good. I don’t often feel the need to add comments (what needs to be said has likely been said already) but this is just so so so good. I know this is a wild thing to say but I believe this may be the highest and best use of chocolate chips.
    I don’t like overly sweet thing but I listened to deb and used the full amount of cinnamon sugar and chocolate chips and i have zero regrets.
    The only change i made was to add about a 1/4 tsp of strong cardamom to the cinnamon sugar. Would do this again.
    Thank you Deb (and ancestors)for this wonderful recipe.

    June 25, 2025 at 12:22 pm Reply
  435. Amanda

    This is delicious and easy. I wanted to see if I could make a smaller batch version and it worked perfectly. I made 1/3 (for 8 servings) and used a 6 inch round pan. Followed instructions exactly but it needed a little less baking time. Sat on the counter for a few days (pressed some foil to seal the exposed edges) and was as good on day 3 as day 1.

    July 18, 2025 at 2:22 pm Reply
  436. Danielle

    I made this exactly as written – all in bowl as noted in updated version. It was fantastic. Would definitely make again. Not too sweet. Just as good the next day.

    July 21, 2025 at 10:20 pm Reply
  437. Sarah

    Hi there, this cake has become a staple in our house- a go to for Christmas and Birthdays. I just noticed this the other day while making it for the 100th time. The recipe ingredient list calls for 1 and 1/2 cup sugar. Then the directions call for the 1 and 1/2 cup sugar to be mixed in with the butter. However, in the end it calls for the remaining 1/2 cup sugar to be mixed with the cinnamon for the sugar topping. Can you please advise? This cake is wonderful, thank you for sharing it with us!

    September 13, 2025 at 12:29 pm Reply
    1. Laura M. Crocenzi

      Hi, the remaining 1/2 cup sugar is at the bottom of the recipe under filling/topping :)

      September 13, 2025 at 12:47 pm Reply
      1. Sarah

        Awesome! Thank you, I totally missed that.

        September 13, 2025 at 1:04 pm Reply
  438. Sue Sober

    Can I bake this in a Bundt pan? Size ?

    October 18, 2025 at 5:23 pm Reply
  439. Nina

    I made this last night. It was a true crowd pleaser. I used a bundt pan instead of the 9 x 13 and it came out perfect. Another outstanding recipe from Smitten Kitchen.

    November 2, 2025 at 11:08 am Reply
  440. Jacqueline

    Love this recipe, I’ve made it at least 5 times!!

    I love the cake, but was thinking of adding the traditional “crumbs” of the coffee cake.

    Do you think this cake is strong enough to hold the weight of crumbs?

    November 25, 2025 at 8:04 am Reply
  441. Lyndsay

    Can. I make this into muffins?

    February 15, 2026 at 8:06 am Reply
